author: jpata
body:
This PR does NOT change any production workflow, it is intended to make sure the MLPF R&D, as submitted to EPJC, is reproducible and usable by the rest of the collaboration.

- Weight file for the MLPF model, accompanying [PFT-15-001]( https://cms.cern.ch/iCMS/analysisadmin/cadilines?line=PFT-25-001&tp=an&id=2958&ancode=PFT-25-001) and the paper version [2601.17554v1](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2601.17554) submitted to EPJC.
- This ONNX weight file corresponds to the case where the self-attention operations are fused to a single op, using [com.microsoft.MultiheadAttention](https://github.com/microsoft/onnxruntime/blob/main/docs/ContribOperators.md#commicrosoftmultiheadattention), which benefits from flash attention on CUDA.
- Additional details for the trained checkpoint are available on [huggingface](https://huggingface.co/jpata/particleflow/tree/main/cms/v2.6.0pre1/pyg-cms_20250722_101813_274478).
- The model consists of 2x3 transformer layers, 16 heads with head dim 16 (embedding size 256), about ~5M parameters in total.
- It was trained on 8x MI250X GPUs over a few days at the LUMI HPC using Run 3 MC (ttbar, QCD and Ztautau with and without PU55-75), as described in the CMS paper and the internal analysis note.
- Needs a corresponding PR on the CMSSW side to update the inference code, I will make this shortly.
title: added weight file for MLPF Run 3 model
